title: Investigate Slow User Creation Process on Signup Callback
story: As a new user signing up for DataTools Pro, I want the Salesforce authentication callback to load quickly, so that I don't abandon the signup process due to a blank screen or long wait.
description:
Investigate why the Salesforce authentication callback page hangs for 30 seconds to 1 minute during first-time signup. The blank white screen during this delay causes users to close the window, contributing to a 20% decrease in activation rate since July/August.

Two areas to evaluate:
1. Check the signup callback for slow-running database queries during user creation
2. Audit the callback page for unnecessary JS libraries (e.g., NewRelic) that may be initializing unnecessarily and slowing page load
acceptance_criteria: Evaluate signup callback for slow queries
Remove unnecessary JS libraries from the callback page
Reduce callback page load time to prevent user abandonment
middleware_changes: Evaluated user creation queries on the callback page. One request to get user region runs on every login.
implementation_notes: Developer investigation found no obviously slow queries. The delay appeared to be on the Salesforce redirect side. No unnecessary JS libraries were found on the callback page. The region lookup runs on every login. Suggested testing together to further diagnose.
comment_summary: Developer investigated and found no slow queries or unnecessary JS libraries. Believes the delay is on the Salesforce redirect. Suggested joint testing.
